comment: Both concepts serve as the root class for all ontology change actions.
Both classes are defined to represent a composition or set of more atomic change actions.
Represents the action of adding a class.
Represents the action of deleting/removing a class.
Represents the addition of an instance/individual.
Represents the deletion/removal of an instance/individual.
Represents the addition of a subClassOf relationship.
Represents the deletion/removal of a subClassOf relationship.
Represents the addition of a subPropertyOf relationship.
Represents the deletion/removal of a subPropertyOf relationship.
Both represent the high-level semantic action of deprecating/obsoleting a resource.
Both represent the action of revoking the deprecated/obsolete status.
`dyn:renC` is specific to classes, while `och:RenameEntity` applies to any OWL entity, making `dyn:renC` a sub-concept.
Both properties refer to the *source* or *prior* ontology version relevant to the change.
Both properties refer to the *target* or *new* ontology version resulting from the change.
